capability_objective: Resolve an exact named-and-located active record amid distractors, use the stable ID returned by search for a dependent full-record retrieval, respect read-only negative constraints, and present selected full-record fields in an exact compact format.
prompt:
I’m reconciling the title register. Work only in this repository and use the genuine local title-register executable for all register access; do not inspect or edit its backing data, audit key, runtime directory, or protected verifier. Invoke it as `python3 title_register.py`; its built-in help documents the interface.

Locate the active title whose exact name is `River Basin Almanac` and whose exact location is `Central`. Search with both that exact name and that exact location. Treat same-name entries elsewhere, near-name entries at Central, and similar IDs as distractors. Only if the exact search returns one match, take the stable ID from that search result and use that returned ID for the dependent full-record retrieval. Do not infer, shorten, edit, or guess an ID, and do not use search snippets as a substitute for the full record.

This reconciliation is read-only. Use no register data operations except that one exact search and its one conditional full-record retrieval. In particular, do not list the register broadly, check availability, change any state, or send a notification. Do not expose unrelated records.

Write the completed reconciliation to `title-reconciliation.md`. It must contain only a compact two-column Markdown table: the header must be exactly `| Field | Value |`, the separator exactly `|---|---|`, and the five rows must be labeled `ID`, `Name`, `Location`, `Status`, and `Date` in that order. Populate every value verbatim from the retrieved full record, end the Date row with exactly one newline, and include no heading, blank line, or prose. Run `python3 protected_tests/verify.py` to verify the deliverable.
